query: Should Polish use single-exe + DLL plugin architecture instead of multiple .exe binaries? Will multi-exe scale as features grow?
refinedQuery: Compare multi-exe two-process architecture (PLAN.md §4) vs single-exe with DLL plugin loading for the Polish Windows maintenance suite, considering privilege boundary, feature scaling, signing cost, open-core enforcement, and real-world precedent.
sections: Rust workspace + shared engine library pattern
Open-core enforcement: separate binary vs DLL

findings.claim: Windows Vista+ isolates services in session 0 with no graphical context. SYSTEM service and user UI CANNOT run in the same process. Single-exe + DLLs cannot replace the multi-exe split for the privilege boundary. This is an OS architectural fact, not a design choice.

Tailscale ships 3 binaries (tailscaled.exe service with internal parent/subprocess split, tailscale.exe CLI, GUI binary). Explicitly split GUI from service in v1.6 for clean separation. WireGuard Windows ships 4+ binaries (installer, wireguard.exe manager+UI, wg.exe CLI, per-tunnel WireGuardTunnel$name services). Firezone uses two-process Tauri with privileged backend. Multi-exe is the convergent pattern in this product category.

findings.subtopic: Signing reputation cost of multiple binaries
findings.claim: Multi-exe does not multiply signing cost much. Per MS Learn + Artifact Signing GA: publisher reputation is anchored to verified identity (not per-binary). File hash reputation seasons per binary but publisher signal carries across all binaries signed by same identity. Recommended: use consistent signing identity across all binaries.

findings.subtopic: Rust workspace with shared library is the no-duplication answer
findings.claim: Cargo workspaces support one shared lib crate (rlib) + multiple bin crates depending on it. Recommended Polish layout: polish-engine (rlib, MIT) + polish-ipc (rlib, MIT) + polish-svc / polish-ui / polish-cli / polish-pro (bin crates). Zero code duplication. Adding a cleanup category = add module to polish-engine, all binaries get it automatically.

findings.subtopic: Rust DLL plugin systems are production-viable but caveat-heavy
findings.claim: abi_stable + libloading enable runtime plugin loading. Caveats: Rust ABI unstable across rustc versions and even compiler runs (abi_stable mitigates via C-ABI boundary); no sandbox (malicious plugin = full system access); plugins must be built with same rustc as host. Production-OK for trusted/signed plugins, NOT for untrusted third-party. Worth considering for v2+ if community extensions become a goal; not needed for v1.0.

findings.subtopic: Open-core enforcement: separate binary vs DLL
findings.claim: Separate proprietary binary = stricter open-core enforcement (unambiguous license boundary). DLL plugin = more flexible integration but requires upfront plugin API design (premature abstraction risk). PLAN.md plan for polish-pro.exe as separate closed-source binary linking MIT polish-engine is the recommended pattern.

keyInsights: EXE count is a function of trust/privilege boundaries; feature count is a function of what's inside the shared engine library. They are orthogonal.
Session 0 isolation since Windows Vista makes single-exe SYSTEM+user impossible. Multi-exe is non-negotiable for the service/UI split.
Tailscale and WireGuard both ship multi-exe in production at large scale — strongest possible counter-evidence to the 'too many exes won't scale' concern.
Rust cargo workspace + shared rlib eliminates code duplication while keeping multi-exe. Best of both worlds for v1.0.
DLL plugin system (abi_stable) is a v2+ feature evaluation, not a v1.0 architectural decision. Premature for current scope.
Publisher reputation in MS Artifact Signing is identity-anchored, not per-certificate, so multi-exe does not linearly multiply seasoning cost.
redFlags: PLAN.md §4 does NOT currently explain WHY multi-exe is required — a future contributor or AI agent might naively suggest collapsing the binaries. Add a one-paragraph rationale citing Session 0 isolation + Tailscale/WireGuard precedent.

PLAN.md §6 Repository layout does NOT yet show the cargo workspace structure with polish-engine as shared rlib. Risk: code duplication starts during implementation. Make the workspace structure explicit before Week 4 build begins.
abi_stable plugin system has no sandbox; if Polish ever wants community plugins, must combine with signing requirement + trust review process.
gaps: No Tauri 2-specific cargo workspace pattern data — generic Cargo workspace info found, but Tauri's bundler/Tauri config + multi-binary workspace needs verification during Week 1-3 spike.
No quantitative polish-engine binary size impact under static linking into 4 binaries vs dynamic. Likely tens of MB; measure during spike.
Plugin API design for v2+ deferred — no concrete spec found because category doesn't have established conventions yet.
Smart App Control multi-binary install completion impact unknown — inferred each binary needs its own file-hash reputation but publisher signal helps.
MSI/NSIS installer flow for 4-binary install not researched in detail — both Tailscale and WireGuard use one installer dropping all binaries.
